description.content: Operations for managing cache artifacts

Check the status of Remote Caching for the authenticated user or team. Returns a JSON-encoded status indicating if Remote Caching is enabled, disabled, or disabled due to usage limits.

Implementers should return `enabled` if the authenticated principal is allowed to use the cache, or `disabled` if not. The `over_limit` and `paused` statuses are optional and can be used to indicate usage limits or temporary suspension.

query.description: The team identifier to perform the request on behalf of. Used for multi-tenant cache implementations where artifacts are scoped to teams.
The team slug to perform the request on behalf of. Alternative to `teamId` for identifying the team.

variable.description: Protocol to use for the remote cache server (This can only be one of http,https)
Hostname and port of the remote cache server

response.name: Remote caching status retrieved successfully

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

Check that a cache artifact with the given `hash` exists. This request returns response headers only and is equivalent to a `GET` request to this endpoint where the response contains no body.

Implementers should return 200 if the artifact exists, or 404 if it does not.

The team slug to perform the request on behalf of. Alternative to `teamId` for identifying the team.

variable.description: (Required) The artifact hash. This is a content-addressable hash that uniquely identifies the cached artifact based on task inputs.
Protocol to use for the remote cache server (This can only be one of http,https)
Hostname and port of the remote cache server

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

Downloads a cache artifact identified by its `hash` specified on the request path. The artifact is downloaded as an octet-stream (binary data).

The artifact data is a gzip-compressed tarball containing the cached task outputs. The client will verify the content-length header against the response body size.

description.content:
Uploads a cache artifact identified by the `hash` specified on the path. The cache artifact can then be downloaded with the provided `hash`.

The request body should contain a gzip-compressed tarball of the task outputs. The server should store this data and make it available for subsequent downloads.

query.description: The team identifier to perform the request on behalf of. Used for multi-tenant cache implementations where artifacts are scoped to teams.
The team slug to perform the request on behalf of. Alternative to `teamId` for identifying the team.

variable.description: (Required) The artifact hash. This is a content-addressable hash that uniquely identifies the cached artifact based on task inputs.
Protocol to use for the remote cache server (This can only be one of http,https)
Hostname and port of the remote cache server

header.description: (Required) The artifact size in bytes
The time taken to generate the artifact in milliseconds. This value is stored and returned when the artifact is downloaded.
A base64-encoded tag for this artifact, typically used for verification. This value is stored and returned when the artifact is downloaded via the `x-artifact-tag` response header.
The git SHA of the HEAD commit at the time this artifact was produced. This value is stored and returned when the artifact is downloaded or checked via the `x-artifact-sha` response header.

A hash summarizing all uncommitted changes (staged, unstaged, and untracked files) in the working tree when this artifact was produced. This value is stored and returned when the artifact is downloaded or checked via the `x-artifact-dirty-hash` response header.
The continuous integration or delivery environment where this artifact operation is being performed. Examples: `github-actions`, `circleci`, `jenkins`.
Indicates whether the client is running in an interactive shell. `1` for interactive, `0` for non-interactive (CI/scripts).

response.name: Artifact uploaded successfully (alternative success response)

variable.description: (Required) The artifact hash. This is a content-addressable hash that uniquely identifies the cached artifact based on task inputs.
Protocol to use for the remote cache server (This can only be one of http,https)
Hostname and port of the remote cache server

The git SHA of the HEAD commit at the time this artifact was produced. This value is stored and returned when the artifact is downloaded or checked via the `x-artifact-sha` response header.

A hash summarizing all uncommitted changes (staged, unstaged, and untracked files) in the working tree when this artifact was produced. This value is stored and returned when the artifact is downloaded or checked via the `x-artifact-dirty-hash` response header.
The continuous integration or delivery environment where this artifact operation is being performed. Examples: `github-actions`, `circleci`, `jenkins`.
Indicates whether the client is running in an interactive shell. `1` for interactive, `0` for non-interactive (CI/scripts).

description.content:
Query information about multiple artifacts by their hashes. Returns metadata about each artifact including size, task duration, and tag.

This endpoint is optional for basic cache functionality but enables optimized cache fetching by allowing the client to query multiple artifacts in a single request.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Operations for recording cache usage analytics

Records cache usage analytics events. The body of this request is an array of cache usage events.

This endpoint is optional but enables cache hit/miss analytics. Implementers can use this data to track cache effectiveness and optimize storage.

Event types:
- `HIT`: A cached artifact was found and used
- `MISS`: No cached artifact was found for the given hash

Source types:
- `LOCAL`: The cache event was on the user's local filesystem cache
- `REMOTE`: The cache event is for the remote cache

description.content: Protocol to use for the remote cache server (This can only be one of http,https)
Hostname and port of the remote cache server

The Turborepo Remote Cache API specification defines the HTTP interface that any remote cache server must implement to be compatible with Turborepo. This spec enables self-hosted remote caching, allowing teams to run their own cache servers.

The remote cache stores build artifacts (outputs from tasks like `build`, `lint`, `test`) identified by content-addressable hashes. When Turborepo encounters a task that matches a previously cached result, it can download the artifact instead of re-executing the task.

Contact Support:
 Name: Turborepo
